name: Dhumavati

visual_attributes.morphology: Old and thin woman with a pale-grey or black complexion
distinctive_features: Rides a horseless chariot or crow
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Unadorned with jewelry, wearing old, dirty clothes

domain_specific_visual.ICONOGRAPHY: Carries a winnowing basket, sometimes a spear or sword, and a skull-cup
domain_specific_visual.SYMBOLS: Crow emblem and banner, garland of severed heads

functional_attributes.primary_function: Represents the fearsome aspect of Mahadevi and cosmic dissolution
secondary_functions: Bestower of boons and supernatural powers

domain_specific_functional.ROLE_IN_MAHAVIDYAS: Seventh Mahavidya, associated with cosmic dissolution
domain_specific_functional.WORSHIP_CONTEXT: Ideal for unpaired members of society and Tantrikas

relationships.description: Dhumavati is one of the ten Mahavidyas
Shares attributes of inauspiciousness and hardship
Shares iconographic similarities and associations with crows

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the void and cosmic dissolution, teaches looking beyond superficial appearances

contrastive_constraints: NOT a young or beautiful goddess
NOT adorned with jewelry or bright clothing
NOT associated with auspicious or prosperous symbols
